messages.content:
Why is data-driven analytics of interest to companies?


It allows companies to make definitive predictions about the future.


It finds creative solutions to business problems without human intervention.


It gives companies the ability to make informed decisions.


It helps Data Analysts shape an analytics problem from a business problem.


I don't know this yet.

Correct answer: It gives companies the ability to make informed decisions. By analyzing data, companies can gain insights and make data-driven decisions rather than relying on intuition or guesswork. This can lead to more effective strategies, better outcomes, and a competitive advantage.

A mobile application delivers market predictions based on stock data from the stock market data platform.

Knowing that the data platform can stream its data, how should the application interact with the data platform to deliver predictions in real time?


connect and export the latest data several times per day


interact programmatically through the data platform API (application programming interface)


interact programmatically, crawling the data platform interface


connect and export the latest data weekly, as daily metrics can be inferred from past computed trends


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is: interact programmatically through the data platform API (application programming interface). By interacting programmatically through the data platform API, the mobile application can receive real-time data updates and predictions based on the latest stock market data. This allows for quicker and more accurate decisions to be made based on the most recent information.

Accenture is helping a large retailer transform their online sales and services. A data analyst audits the client’s customer journey and maps out the kind of data they need to collect at each touch point.

How does this help the client achieve their objectives?


by increasing their customer knowledge and leveraging that information to improve customer experience


by updating their sales platform and adding new features


by identifying products that are poor sellers and removing them from the inventory


by shifting the focus away from the customer and towards new products or services


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is: by increasing their customer knowledge and leveraging that information to improve customer experience. By auditing the client's customer journey and mapping out the required data at each touchpoint, the data analyst helps the client gain a better understanding of their customers' behavior, preferences, and pain points. This knowledge can then be leveraged to improve the customer experience and create more personalized and relevant offerings. By improving the customer experience, the client can achieve their objective of increasing online sales and services.

How does this help the client achieve their objectives?


by increasing their customer knowledge and leveraging that information to improve customer experience


by updating their sales platform and adding new features


by identifying products that are poor sellers and removing them from the inventory


by shifting the focus away from the customer and towards new products or services


I don't know this yet.

The correct answer is: by increasing their customer knowledge and leveraging that information to improve customer experience. By auditing the client's customer journey and mapping out the required data at each touchpoint, the data analyst helps the client gain a better understanding of their customers' behavior, preferences, and pain points. This knowledge can then be leveraged to improve the customer experience and create more personalized and relevant offerings. By improving the customer experience, the client can achieve their objective of increasing online sales and services.

Everyone within a company needs to be aware of what data can do to improve business processes and how to make it happen.

Which concept is referred to in the statement above?


data culture


data strategy


data literacy


data governance


I don't know this yet.

The concept referred to in the statement above is "data literacy." Data literacy means having the ability to read, understand, create, and communicate data. By improving data literacy across the organization, employees can become more aware of how data can be used to improve business processes, and how to make data-informed decisions. This involves training on data analysis, visualization, and management, as well as developing a culture that values and prioritizes data-driven decision-making.

Accenture is involved in the transformation of an organization into a data-driven and intelligent enterprise.

Which ability does Accenture need to ensure the organization has to become a successful business?


to collect exabytes and zettabytes of data from different sources


to gather, re-organize, and store different categories of all raw data for easy and faster retrieval


to store large amounts of data including raw and unorganized facts


to capture, curate, and consume the right data sets


I don't know this yet.

The ability that Accenture needs to ensure the organization has to become a successful business is "to capture, curate, and consume the right data sets." Collecting and storing large amounts of data is important, but it is equally important to capture the relevant data that can provide valuable insights and inform decision-making. By capturing, curating, and consuming the right data sets, an organization can become more data-driven and make informed business decisions based on insights and trends.

The Department Manager researches new data platforms for the company and requests a list of essential features.

Which essential features should the list include?


centralization, dashboards, programmatic access


decentralization, programmatic access, strong security


dashboards, decentralization, strong security


strong security, centralization, programmatic access


I don't know this yet.

The essential features that the list should include are: centralization, dashboards, programmatic access. 

Centralization refers to having all the data in one place, making it easier to manage and access. Dashboards allow for visualizing data trends and performance indicators. Programmatic access enables seamless integration between the data platform and other systems. By having these essential features, the data platform can provide a unified and efficient way to manage, visualize, and access data for the company. 

Decentralization can lead to data silos and complexity, and strong security is important but can be considered non-essential depending on the company's needs and data sensitivity.

What function do regulations like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) serve?


to enforce the protection of consumer data through criminal penalties against non-compliant corporations


to ensure companies are safeguarding customer data according to a set of minimum standards


to allow government agencies access to companies' customer data in the case of criminal proceedings


to encourage companies to invest in data protection through compliance subsidies


I don't know this yet.

The function of regulations like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is "to ensure companies are safeguarding customer data according to a set of minimum standards." GDPR is a regulation established by the European Union (EU) that sets guidelines for the collection, processing, and storage of personal data of individuals within the EU. The main goal of GDPR is to protect the privacy and data of individuals and to ensure that companies are accountable for how they collect and use personal data. Non-compliance with GDPR can lead to significant financial penalties for companies. Therefore, it is important for companies to comply with GDPR and other data protection regulations to ensure the privacy and security of customer data.

to enforce the protection of consumer data through criminal penalties against non-compliant corporations


to ensure companies are safeguarding customer data according to a set of minimum standards


to allow government agencies access to companies' customer data in the case of criminal proceedings


to encourage companies to invest in data protection through compliance subsidies


I don't know this yet.

The function of regulations like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is "to ensure companies are safeguarding customer data according to a set of minimum standards." GDPR is a regulation established by the European Union (EU) that sets guidelines for the collection, processing, and storage of personal data of individuals within the EU. The main goal of GDPR is to protect the privacy and data of individuals and to ensure that companies are accountable for how they collect and use personal data. Non-compliance with GDPR can lead to significant financial penalties for companies. Therefore, it is important for companies to comply with GDPR and other data protection regulations to ensure the privacy and security of customer data.

Which comprehensive approach should companies follow to trust and use data as a critical differentiator?


Create awareness on the importance of data in business among employees.


Establish methods to capture data at high speeds using technology.


Develop clear data governance policies with a strong data strategy.


Identify strong strategies to consume data in ways that were not possible before.


I don't know this yet.

The comprehensive approach that companies should follow to trust and use data as a critical differentiator is: Develop clear data governance policies with a strong data strategy. 

A data governance policy defines how an organization collects, uses, stores, and manages data across the organization. A strong data strategy outlines how an organization plans to use data to achieve its business objectives and gain a competitive edge. By developing clear data governance policies and a strong data strategy, companies can ensure that everyone in the organization has a shared understanding of data, how it can be used, and how it should be managed. This approach helps to build trust in the data and ensures that the data is being used as a critical differentiator. Establishing methods to capture data at high speeds using technology, creating awareness on the importance of data in business among employees, and identifying strong strategies to consume data in ways that were not possible before are also important, but building a comprehensive data governance and data strategy framework is the foundation for achieving these goals.

Which technologies combine to make data a critical organizational asset?


Penetration Testing and Intelligence Practice


Internet of Things (IoT) and electronic devices


Speech and Natural Language Processing (NLP)


Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence (AI)


I don't know this yet.

The technologies that combine to make data a critical organizational asset are: Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence (AI). 

Machine learning and AI enable organizations to automate and streamline workflows, gain insights from large volumes of data, and improve decision-making. With machine learning and AI, organizations can detect patterns, anomalies, and trends that may be difficult to see with traditional data analysis methods. Additionally, speech and natural language processing (NLP) and Internet of Things (IoT) are tools that can be utilized as part of machine learning and AI applications. Penetration testing and intelligence practice are not directly related to utilizing data as a critical organizational asset.

How does Accenture help companies harness the power of data to achieve optimal business outcomes?


They develop data governance frameworks that go on to form the basis of the company's data analytics strategy.


They help companies think about and define the "art of the possible" with their data assets.


They utilize only their data and analytics platform to enable companies.


They provide an interactive front end for a database solution that changes the data culture.


I don't know this yet.

Accenture helps companies harness the power of data to achieve optimal business outcomes by helping them think about and define the "art of the possible" with their data assets. Accenture utilizes their expertise to help companies identify opportunities to leverage their data to improve business processes, anticipate and respond to market trends, and create competitive advantages. They work with companies to develop strategies for data governance, data analytics, and data management that meet their specific needs. Additionally, Accenture provides various data services, including data architecture, management, analysis, and visualization, to help companies achieve their desired outcomes.

How can the adoption of a data platform simplify data governance for an organization?


by checking the integrity of the data that is currently stored in the organization's data lake


by monitoring compliance with data protection and data privacy requirements


by implementing models that predict compliance with data protection and data privacy policies


by managing data collection, so better decisions can be made about data cleaning or pre-processing


I don't know this yet.

The adoption of a data platform can simplify data governance for an organization by managing data collection, so better decisions can be made about data cleaning or pre-processing. 

A data platform consolidates and centralizes data from multiple sources, making it easier to manage, process, and analyze. By using a data platform, organizations can ensure that data governance policies are enforced consistently across all data sources. This simplifies the task of data governance, as it ensures that data is collected and managed in a compliant manner. Additionally, the use of a data platform can support the creation of data lineage, metadata management, and data catalogs.

How should a common data source, like social media comments, be categorized


structured data


dirty data


temporary data


unstructured data


I don't know this yet.

A common data source like social media comments would be categorized as "unstructured data." 

Unstructured data refers to data that does not have a predefined data model or organization, and is typically not stored in a traditional database table. Social media comments are typically textual data that do not follow a structured format and are characterized by diverse language use, grammar, and context. These types of data require special techniques such as natural language processing (NLP) and text analytics to extract meaningful insights. Categorizing social media comments as unstructured data highlights the need for advanced data processing techniques such as NLP.

What is an example of Big Data?


providing real-time data feeds on millions of people with wearable devices


tracking the work hours of 100 employees with a real-time dashboard


sending user survey responses from various store branches to a single, central database


entering and tracking a company's daily transaction records in a spreadsheet


I don't know this yet.

An example of Big Data is: providing real-time data feeds on millions of people with wearable devices. 

Big Data refers to extremely large and complex data sets that can be too big to be processed and analyzed by traditional data processing tools and methods. Providing real-time data feeds on millions of people with wearable devices generates large volumes of data that require specialized hardware, software, and analytical tools to store, process, and analyze. This enables companies to collect and analyze large amounts of data to gain insights into customer behavior, preferences, and trends, and make data-driven decisions. Tracking the work hours of 100 employees, sending user survey responses from various store branches to a single, central database, and entering and tracking a company's daily transaction records in a spreadsheet may involve data processing, but they are not considered Big Data due to the relatively small amount of data being processed.

What does Accenture help businesses implement to ensure their data is trustworthy and reliable?


data and analytics platforms


data culture and a growth mindset


data governance and management policies


data citizenship and literacy programs


I don't know this yet.

Accenture helps businesses implement "data governance and management policies" to ensure their data is trustworthy and reliable. 

Data governance refers to the process of managing the availability, usability, integrity, security, and privacy of data used in an organization. It involves developing and implementing policies, procedures, and standards to ensure the proper management of data throughout its lifecycle. Accenture works with businesses to develop and implement data governance frameworks and policies that support their data strategy and objectives. This ensures that data is managed effectively and that it can be trusted and relied upon for informed decision-making. Data and analytics platforms, data culture and a growth mindset, and data citizenship and literacy programs are also important aspects of building a data-driven organization, but data governance and management policies are the foundation for ensuring that data is trustworthy and reliable.

What is an advantage of using a fully-integrated cloud-based data analytics platform?


It provides the computing power needed to convert raw data into meaningful information for decision makers.


It ensures the accurate and timely collection of data at the source.


It eliminates the client's responsibility for security by transferring this function to the Cloud Vendor.


It improves the quality of data and the data literacy of all employees.


I don't know this yet.

An advantage of using a fully-integrated cloud-based data analytics platform is that it provides the computing power needed to convert raw data into meaningful information for decision-makers. 

Cloud-based data analytics platforms typically provide scalable computing power and storage capabilities that enable organizations to process large volumes of data in a faster and more efficient manner. It also eliminates the need for organizations to invest in and manage their own IT infrastructure, which can be costly and time-consuming. By leveraging the power of a cloud-based platform, organizations can focus on analyzing the data and gaining insights that can drive informed decision-making. While cloud vendors do offer security features, ultimately the responsibility for data security remains with the organization. Improving data quality and data literacy are important goals that can be achieved through analytics platforms, but they are not direct advantages of using a cloud-based platform.

Which describes a platform?


a piece of technology that connects people and other technologies together  


a framework that provides foundational working system for software development


a ready-to-use software package that performs a specific business function


an organized collection of data stored electronically on a local computer system or cloud


I don't know this yet.

A platform is "a framework that provides a foundational working system for software development." 

Platforms are software systems that provide a framework for other software programs or applications to be built on. These platforms typically provide a set of tools, APIs, and structure to facilitate the development and deployment of software applications. They provide a foundation for software developers to build and deploy new applications efficiently and effectively. This can include operating systems, cloud platforms, development frameworks, and more. While some platforms may also include ready-to-use software packages that perform specific business functions, the main focus of a platform is to provide a foundational framework for software development.

What differentiates Accenture’s Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) when our clients are making a decision to partner with a services company?


an ability to automate the product lifecycle for improved time to market and customer experience


an ability to create custom standalone applications and software specific to the business


an ability to use pre-packaged software solutions from platform partners right out of the box


an ability to integrate deep technical knowledge, functional context, and industry experience


I don't know this yet.

What differentiates Accenture's Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) when clients are making a decision to partner with a services company is: an ability to integrate deep technical knowledge, functional context, and industry experience.

IPS is designed to simplify the use of software platforms that enable clients to drive insights from their data. Accenture combines deep technical knowledge, functional context, and industry expertise to deliver platform-based solutions that are customized for each client's unique needs and objectives. Accenture works with clients to integrate software platforms into their existing IT infrastructure, and to develop, deploy, and manage applications and services that leverage platform capabilities. By leveraging their expertise across multiple industries and technology domains, Accenture can design, build, and deploy end-to-end platform solutions that help clients remain competitive and drive business outcomes. Automated product lifecycles, custom standalone applications, and pre-packaged software solutions from platform partners can be part of the IPS offering but the main differentiator is the integration of deep technical knowledge, functional context, and industry experience.
